context: Tests whether the Vision framework skill corrects modern Swift Vision APIs while preserving legacy VN, VisionKit, and Core ML sibling boundaries.

checklist.name: Modern versus legacy boundary
checklist.description: Explains that new iOS 18+ Vision code should use Swift-native request and observation types with async `perform(on:)`, and keeps legacy `VN*` requests, handlers, conversion helpers, and method labels scoped to legacy code.

checklist.name: Barcode and geometry types
checklist.description: Uses `BarcodeSymbology` with `DetectBarcodesRequest`, stores modern observation bounds as `NormalizedRect`, and converts for display with `toImageCoordinates(_:origin:)` or equivalent modern geometry APIs rather than `VNImageRectForNormalizedRect`.

checklist.name: Object tracking correction
checklist.description: Seeds modern object tracking with `DetectedObjectObservation(boundingBox:)` or another bounding-box-providing observation and `TrackObjectRequest(detectedObject:)`, preserves the request across frames, and does not set `trackingLevel` on modern `TrackObjectRequest`.

checklist.name: Instance mask correction
checklist.description: Calls modern `InstanceMaskObservation.generateMask(for:)` and keeps legacy `generateMask(forInstances:)` or related `VNInstanceMaskObservation` methods out of modern snippets.

checklist.name: Horizon and saliency result shapes
checklist.description: Handles `DetectHorizonRequest` as returning one `HorizonObservation` with `angle: Measurement<UnitAngle>` and handles attention/objectness saliency requests as returning one `SaliencyImageObservation`, not arrays requiring `first`.

checklist.name: VisionKit scanner boundary
checklist.description: For `DataScannerViewController`, checks both `isSupported` and `isAvailable`, includes camera permission guidance, starts scanning after presentation, and keeps VisionKit barcode symbologies on the `VNBarcodeSymbology` side rather than replacing them with `BarcodeSymbology`.

checklist.name: Core ML handoff
checklist.description: Uses Vision only to run an already-prepared model through `CoreMLRequest` or `VNCoreMLRequest`, notes that `CoreMLRequest` result observations vary by model output shape when relevant, and routes conversion, profiling, optimization, deployment packaging, and lifecycle decisions to the `coreml` skill instead of expanding them.

checklist.name: Output scope and evidence
checklist.description: Provides concise corrected Swift snippets and review guidance without creating a project, inventing unsupported APIs, or broadening into unrelated sibling skills.
